summary: Gruber documents how the Los Angeles Times, under owner Patrick Soon-Shiong, fundamentally altered a contributor's op-ed about RFK Jr. from a critical piece into one with an optimistic tone toward Trump's healthcare agenda. Eric Reinhart's original column argued that RFK Jr.'s disregard for science would inflict preventable death, but the LA Times rewrote it to suggest Trump's healthcare disruption could pay off. Gruber contrasts the original and published versions side by side to demonstrate the editorial malpractice. He then adds a technical discovery: his Markdown tools revealed that the LA Times editors left behind a vestigial HTML link from Reinhart's original draft, providing forensic proof of the editorial tampering. Gruber frames this as further evidence that the LA Times has become a propaganda outlet competing with the Washington Post to capitulate to Trump.
key_points: The LA Times radically altered Eric Reinhart's op-ed from a critique of RFK Jr. into a piece suggesting Trump's healthcare disruption could be positive
The original headline was 'RFK Jr's Wrecking Ball Won't Fix Public Health' while the LA Times ran it as 'Trump's Healthcare Disruption Could Pay Off — if He Pushes Real Reform'
Gruber's homegrown Markdown tools inadvertently uncovered forensic evidence of the editing — an invisible vestigial hyperlink left in the HTML from Reinhart's original draft
This incident is part of a pattern of Soon-Shiong's editorial interference that Gruber has been tracking since before the 2024 election
The New Republic agreed to publish Reinhart's piece as originally intended
money_quotes: My only question here is whether Reinhart really believed the LA Times op-ed page was going to run his piece as written.

Editing out the most urgent point of an OpEd in the minutes before sending to press while then also assigning a title and image that suggest an argument entirely opposite to the author's clear intent is pretty shitty.
Whoever at the LA Times edited Reinhart's column to pervert its meaning, did so sloppily, leaving behind at least this one acerbic vestigial link from his originally-submitted prose.
The Los Angeles Times — currently in a race to the bottom with The Washington Post to determine which of the two venerable institutions can sell out to Donald Trump the hardest.
You can't see the link or click on it as rendered in a browser, because there's no content between the opening and closing tags.
Turns out, the LA Times itself effectively published proof of its 'editing' that completely perverted Reinhart's intended thrust.
key_insight: The LA Times under Soon-Shiong has devolved into a propaganda outlet, and its own sloppy HTML editing left forensic evidence of how it perverted an anti-RFK Jr. op-ed into pro-Trump pablum.
